event_date: Date of the event, cast to DATE from the original YYYYMMDD string
event_datetime: Event datetime converted to the configured timezone from event_timestamp (or custom timestamp if configured)
event_timestamp: Time in microseconds (UTC) when the event was received by Google Analytics
event_custom_timestamp: Event timestamp in microseconds derived from a custom event parameter (e.g. collected via Date.now()), falling back to event_timestamp when the custom parameter is null. Only present when customTimestampParam is configured
event_name: Name of the event (e.g. page_view, purchase, scroll)
session_id: Unique session identifier, constructed by concatenating user_pseudo_id with the ga_session_id event parameter
user_pseudo_id: Pseudonymous user identifier (e.g. app instance ID or client ID) assigned when the user first opens the app or visits the site
user_id: Last non-null user_id observed within the session, ordered by timestamp. Reflects the most recent authenticated identity for the session
merged_user_id: Coalesced identifier: uses the session-level user_id if available, otherwise falls back to user_pseudo_id
page_location: Full page URL extracted from the page_location event parameter

page.description: Parsed page details struct derived from the page_location event parameter

columns.hostname: Hostname extracted from the page URL (e.g. www.example.com)
columns.path: URL path component with scheme, hostname, query string, and fragment removed
columns.query: Raw query string including the leading '?' character, if present

query_params.description: Parsed query parameters as an array of key-value pairs

landing_page.description: Session landing page: the first page struct (where entrances > 0) within the session, ordered by timestamp

columns.query: Query string of the landing page

query_params.description: Parsed query parameters of the landing page URL

event_params.description: Event parameters array. Excluded parameters (from excludedEventParams and promoted parameters) are filtered out

value.description: A record containing the event parameter value

columns.string_value: String value of the event parameter
columns.int_value: Integer value of the event parameter
columns.float_value: Float value of the event parameter (currently unused in GA4)
columns.double_value: Double value of the event parameter

session_params.description: Session-level parameters aggregated from event_params. For each configured session parameter, the last non-null value within the session is kept. Empty array if no sessionParams are configured

value.description: A record containing the session parameter value

columns.string_value: String value of the session parameter
columns.int_value: Integer value of the session parameter
columns.float_value: Float value of the session parameter (currently unused in GA4)
columns.double_value: Double value of the session parameter

value.description: A record containing the user property value

columns.string_value: String value of the user property
columns.int_value: Integer value of the user property
columns.double_value: Double value of the user property
columns.float_value: Float value of the user property (currently unused in GA4)
columns.set_timestamp_micros: Time in microseconds at which the user property was last set

ecommerce.description: Ecommerce data with fixes applied: transaction_id is nullified when set to '(not set)', and purchase_revenue is corrected for NaN values and a legacy GA4 bug where it was missing on purchase events (falls back to the 'value' event parameter)

columns.total_item_quantity: Total number of items in this event (sum of items.quantity)
columns.purchase_revenue_in_usd: Purchase revenue in USD. Populated for purchase events only
columns.purchase_revenue: Purchase revenue in local currency, with NaN and missing-value fixes applied
columns.refund_value_in_usd: Refund amount in USD. Populated for refund events only
columns.refund_value: Refund amount in local currency. Populated for refund events only
columns.transaction_id: Transaction ID of the ecommerce transaction. Nullified when the original value is '(not set)'
columns.unique_items: Number of unique items in this event, based on item_id, item_name, and item_brand

items.description: Array of items associated with the event (e.g. products in a purchase or product list)

columns.quantity: Quantity of the item (defaults to 1 if not specified)
columns.item_revenue_in_usd: Item revenue in USD (price_in_usd * quantity). Purchase events only
columns.item_revenue: Item revenue in local currency (price * quantity). Purchase events only
columns.item_refund_in_usd: Item refund in USD. Refund events only
columns.item_refund: Item refund in local currency. Refund events only
columns.coupon: Coupon code applied to the item
columns.affiliation: Product affiliation (e.g. supplying company or store location)
columns.item_list_id: ID of the list in which the item was presented
columns.item_list_name: Name of the list in which the item was presented
columns.item_list_index: Position of the item in a list
columns.creative_name: Name of the creative used in a promotional spot

user_ltv.description: Lifetime value record for the user. Not populated in intraday tables

columns.revenue: Lifetime value revenue of the user
columns.currency: Lifetime value currency of the user

collected_traffic_source.description: Traffic source data collected with the event, including UTM parameters and click identifiers

columns.manual_source: Manual source (utm_source), also includes parsed referral parameters
columns.manual_medium: Manual medium (utm_medium), also includes parsed referral parameters

session_first_traffic_source.description: The data extracted from the collected_traffic_source struct of the session's first event. Can be used for building custom attribution models.

columns.manual_source: Manual source (utm_source), also includes parsed referral parameters
columns.manual_medium: Manual medium (utm_medium), also includes parsed referral parameters

session_traffic_source_last_click.description: Primary session attribution data. Session-level last non-direct click attribution.

columns.campaign_id: Campaign ID of the last clicked manual campaign
columns.campaign_name: Name of the last clicked manual campaign
columns.source: Source of the last clicked manual campaign
columns.medium: Medium of the last clicked manual campaign
columns.term: Keyword/term of the last clicked manual campaign
columns.content: Content metadata of the last clicked manual campaign
columns.source_platform: Platform of the last clicked manual campaign
columns.creative_format: Creative format of the last clicked manual campaign
columns.marketing_tactic: Marketing tactic of the last clicked manual campaign

cross_channel_campaign.description: Last-click cross-channel campaign attribution. The primary session attribution data within the session_traffic_source_last_click struct

columns.primary_channel_group: The default dimension for ranking traffic popularity and acquisition performance. Prefer this over source/medium for general summaries. Use this as the default dimension for all general 'traffic source' questions
columns.default_channel_group: Default channel group for the session's last non-direct click

user_traffic_source.description: Traffic source that first acquired the user. Sourced from the GA4 export traffic_source field. Not populated in intraday tables

columns.name: Name of the marketing campaign that first acquired the user
columns.medium: Medium that first acquired the user (e.g. paid search, organic, referral)
columns.source: Source network that first acquired the user

event_previous_timestamp: Time in microseconds (UTC) when the previous event happened
event_value_in_usd: Currency-converted value (in USD) of the event's 'value' parameter
event_bundle_sequence_id: Sequential ID of the bundle in which the event was uploaded
event_server_timestamp_offset: Timestamp offset between collection time and upload time in microseconds

privacy_info.description: Consent status information for the user when consent mode is enabled

columns.ads_storage: Whether ad targeting/storage is enabled (Yes, No, or Unset)
columns.analytics_storage: Whether Analytics storage is enabled (Yes, No, or Unset)
columns.uses_transient_token: Whether measurement uses transient tokens instead of cookies (Yes, No, or Unset)

user_first_touch_timestamp: Time in microseconds at which the user first opened the app or visited the site

device.description: Information about the device from which the event originated

columns.mobile_os_hardware_model: Device model retrieved directly from the operating system
columns.vendor_id: IDFV (present only if IDFA is not collected)
columns.is_limited_ad_tracking: Whether the device has Limit Ad Tracking enabled

columns.hostname: Hostname associated with the logged event

geo.description: Geographic location where the event was initiated, based on IP address

columns.continent: Continent from which the event was reported
columns.sub_continent: Subcontinent from which the event was reported
columns.country: Country from which the event was reported
columns.region: Region from which the event was reported
columns.metro: Metro area from which the event was reported
columns.city: City from which the event was reported

app_info.description: Information about the app in which the event was initiated

columns.id: Package name or bundle ID of the app
columns.firebase_app_id: Firebase App ID associated with the app
columns.version: App versionName (Android) or short bundle version (iOS)

stream_id: Numeric ID of the data stream from which the event originated
platform: Data stream platform (Web, iOS, or Android)

columns.hostname: Hostname where the event was recorded

is_active_user: Whether the user was active at any point during the calendar day. Only populated in daily tables
batch_event_index: Sequential order of the event within a batch, based on occurrence on the device
batch_page_id: Sequential number assigned to each page, incrementing within an engagement
batch_ordering_id: Monotonically increasing number incremented per network request from a given page

publisher.description: Publisher data from AdMob integration. Not populated in intraday or Fresh Daily tables

columns.ad_revenue_in_usd: Estimated ad revenue in USD. Populated for ad impression events only
columns.ad_format: Ad format (e.g. Interstitial, Banner, Rewarded, Native advanced)
columns.ad_source_name: Source network that served the ad (e.g. AdMob Network, Meta Audience Network)

row_inserted_timestamp: Timestamp of when the row was inserted or last refreshed by the incremental pipeline
data_is_final: Whether the data is considered final and not expected to change. Determined by export type (intraday/fresh = not final) or by a configurable day threshold since event_date
export_type: GA4 export type the event originated from: 'daily', 'intraday', or 'fresh'
